description_long: This object is a historical artillery cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of aged, patinated metal, featuring decorative rings and a slightly flared muzzle. The carriage is constructed from heavy wooden planks, reinforced with metal brackets, and is supported by four solid wooden wheels. Metal fastenings and bolts are visible along the carriage, and there are two prominent lifting rings on the top of the barrel. The surface of the cannon and carriage shows signs of weathering, with a greenish-blue patina on the metal and rough, worn wood. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for deployment in historical urban or battlefield settings.
description: A weathered, patinated metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden wheeled carriage, featuring decorative rings and metal fastenings. The object has a robust, historical appearance with visible signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon barrel and the symmetrical arrangement of the wheels and carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the four wheels, with visible lifting rings and decorative barrel details.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the breech of the cannon, the back of the carriage, and the alignment of the wheels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the length of the barrel and carriage details from the other side.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed large-caliber cannon barrel mounted centrally on the carriage; no additional mounts visible.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the carriage and barrel are exposed with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the cannon has a solid greenish patina and the carriage is natural wood.
